mnemonics: CISCO: Categorize It Simply, Choose One. (Remember the main categories: Complexity, Industry, Size, Change, Orientation)
keyPoints: Software projects can be categorized by their size, complexity, and criticality (e.g., small, medium, large).
They can also be grouped by the type of product or application domain (e.g., web, mobile, embedded, AI/ML).
Another key categorization is by the development approach or business model (e.g., in-house, outsourced, fixed-price, time-and-materials).
commonMistakes: Assuming one project management methodology (like Agile or Waterfall) fits all project categories.
Overlooking the impact of a project's category on its risk, cost, and schedule estimation.
Treating a highly complex, novel project with the same processes as a simple, well-understood one.
